LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE ("DEFENDANT") WITHIN THE TIME ALLOWED AFTER DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER WAS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL AS TO THIS DEFENDANT IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 581 (F)(2). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE THAT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER BREACHED A SPECIFIC MANDATORY DUTY, WHICH THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT NOW DO. (SCHWARTZ V. POIZNER (2010) 187 CAL. APP. 4TH 572). A WRIT OF MANDATE CANNOT BE BASED ON GENERAL ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OR STATUTES INVOLVING THE DEFENDANT'S EXERCISE OF DISCRETION. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO GIVEN TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT ABUSED HIS DISCRETION BY ACTING ARBITRARILY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-05-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1403 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE's Motion To Dismiss is Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff failed to amend the complaint as to Defendant Commissioner of California Department of Insurance ("Defendant") within the time allowed after defendant's demurrer was sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The motion for a judgment of dismissal as to this Defendant is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 581 (f)(2). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Continued To Jul-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANNE MICHELLE BURR, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-1403 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DAVE JONES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff is also given ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
